params.prompt: Transform this room image into a cinematic editorial real-estate still. Preserve exact architecture, perspective, walls, windows, door openings, furniture layout, lens realism, and room scale. Maintain believable residential scale for a 11.917 by 11.667 ft Nest. Soft morning directional light from bay window; boost midtones slightly for readability while preserving sculpted shadows. Use warm practical lamp for evening shots. A calm, family-friendly Japandi interior that balances warm neutrals, low-profile furniture, and layered natural textures. Prioritize a cozy work corner in the bedroom while keeping sightlines clean across the open-plan living, dining, and kitchen. Light is soft and directional from windows; materials focus on light oak, warm walnut accents, hand-glazed ceramics, woven pendants, and tactile textiles. Keep these fixed unless the user explicitly wants transformation: exact architecture room geometry furniture layout lens realism vertical line integrity believable scale light entering from visible sources only VISUAL CONSISTENCY REQUIREMENT (reference image provided): The Dine visible through the open-plan dining area must show exactly the same dining table, pendant light above the dining area, dining chairs, round light-oak dining table — round 48in diameter, light oak veneer tabletop (#D2BFA3), slim tapered legs, matte finish, oversized woven pendant — dome-shaped woven seagrass pendant, warm tan (#B58E61), diameter 28in, interior white diffuser, one hand-glazed tan ceramic vase, one small ceramic vase (tan) and linen napkin styling as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. The Taste visible through the open kitchen beyond the dining area must show exactly the same kitchen cabinets, range hood, kitchen island or counter, small ceramic canister group in tan (#B58E61), round light-oak dining table — round 48in diameter, light oak veneer tabletop (#D2BFA3), slim tapered legs, matte finish, visible edge of round light-oak dining table spec (see shared objects), small ceramic canister group in tan (#B58E61) near the sink as in the reference image — do not invent different furniture. The sofa, coffee table, area rug, low-profile 3-seater sofa — pale oat linen upholstery (#EDE6DE), low wooden plinth base in light oak (#D2BFA3), single long seat cushion, no visible legs, textured wool rug — warm oatmeal (#EDE6DE) with subtle charcoal linear weave (#2F3230), 8x10ft, low pile, low organic coffee table — organic-shaped low coffee table, solid walnut stained warm brown (#8A6E52), matte finish, curved edges, tall potted fiddle-leaf fig — matte terra-cotta pot in muted green (#6A736A), height ~5ft, round light-oak dining table — round 48in diameter, light oak veneer tabletop (#D2BFA3), slim tapered legs, matte finish, oversized woven pendant — dome-shaped woven seagrass pendant, warm tan (#B58E61), diameter 28in, interior white diffuser, bookshelf in this room must exactly match the style, materials, and form shown in reference image — treat it as the definitive source of truth for these pieces.
